content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Advice File

Date: Nov. 16, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Sarah Cameron

Subject: Real Estate company as source of income to agent who works as an independent contractor.

Tony Fisher of the Santa Barbara City Attorney's Office called today with the following fact situation:

A city planning commissioner's wife works for a real estate company on a contract basis. They provide her with an office, furniture, a phone-answering and message service and access to their listings. They provide no fringe benefits. In return, she pays them a straight 4% of her commissions.

The real estate company is assisting the owner of an apartment complex in completing the process of converting the apartments into condominiums. Although the firm does not now have a contract to sell the condominiums, it anticipates such a contract if the conversion is allowed. There are approximately 200 units in the complex.

Can the planning commissioner vote on the conversion? On the question of foreseeability, I referred him to the standards enunciated in the Thorner opinion, i.e. likelihood and substantial probability. I advised that I thought it was foreseeable that the real estate company would get the contract to sell the condos if the conversion is allowed. Fisher seemed to have more of a problem with foreseeability.

On the question of whether the real estate company is a source of income to the spouse, I said that we had no formulated staff policy on this question, but that I thought that the firm was a source because she receives her income through her relationship to the firm. I also noted that this was a situation in which it seemed like bias might be created because of the spouse's relationship to the real estate company so that that might tip the scales in a close case.
